results.title: Vitamin C
results.creator_credit: Michael Coyle and British Broadcasting Corporation
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Home Economics”
During the winter many of us suffer from a lack of Vitamin C. What foods are the best sources of Vitamin C and how can we preserve it in our cooking?

results.title: Fats
results.creator_credit: Michael Coyle and British Broadcasting Corporation
results.brief_description:
Episode number 1 of Series “Home Economics”.
This film stresses the correlation between heart disease and the consumption of fats and oils in the diet. Outlines ways in which the amount of fat and oil in the diet can be reduced.

results.creator_credit: Sally Methven and University of Queensland. Dept. of Human Movement Studies
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Hockey film”.
The second in a series of films in which well-known international women’s hockey players demonstrate their skills. The basic skills of push pass, push in, attacking and defensive dodges, tackling, jab and Lunges are shown.

results.title: A Foreign Affair
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “High Office”.
A dictator comes to power in a former British colony. Conditions in his country deteriorate but business opportunities for Britain are good. The program examines how the British government might view the request for a royal visit and how it would react to rumours of an impending coup against the new regime
